art_market.summary: Art Basel Hong Kong 2026 is in full swing (Mar 27-29) at HKCEC, following two strong VIP preview days. Galleries report solid sales and optimistic sentiment despite global economic headwinds.
top_stories: Fair features 240 galleries from 41 countries; significant depth of demand for Asia-Pacific artists noted.
New 'Echoes' and 'Zero 10' curatorial sectors drawing major engagement from collectors.
Sales highlights include works by George Condo, Louise Bourgeois, and Lee Bul, with strong museum and private collection interest.

stock_market.summary: KOSPI volatile; opened down ~3% (5,220.10) but pared losses in afternoon trading. Markets under pressure from Google's 'TurboQuant' announcement and Iran-US geopolitical tensions.
key_factors: Google's 'TurboQuant' AI compression tech triggers sell-off in global memory chip stocks (Samsung, SK Hynix down ~4%).
Geopolitical uncertainty surrounding US-Iran conflict continues to dampen investor sentiment.
Retail buying in the afternoon helped the KOSPI recover some losses, reclaiming the 5,300 mark.
